evals.name: setup-new-project
evals.prompt: Set up shared skills for my project at ~/RepoBase/test-project. The project already has a .claude/settings.json with some permissions configured.
evals.expected_output: The project's .claude/settings.json is updated with additionalDirectories and SessionStart hook, preserving existing settings

evals.name: list-available-skills
evals.prompt: What shared skills are available? List them all with their descriptions.
evals.expected_output: A list of all skills in the shared library with names and trigger descriptions

evals.name: diagnose-missing-skill
evals.prompt: I added shared-skills to my project but the mermaid skill doesn't show up in /skills. Help me figure out why.
evals.expected_output: Agent checks additionalDirectories config, verifies paths, runs verification script, and identifies the issue

assertions.text: Suggests running library-verify or the verification script
Mentions SKILL.md must be one level deep

evals.name: remove-submodule-migration
evals.prompt: My cowork project still uses a git submodule for shared-skills at .claude/skills/shared. Migrate it to the additionalDirectories approach.
evals.expected_output: Agent removes the submodule, adds additionalDirectories, removes duplicate skills, adds verification hook

assertions.text: Runs git rm on the submodule path

evals.name: add-new-skill-to-library
evals.prompt: I want to add a new skill called 'api-docs' to the shared library. It should trigger when users ask to generate API documentation from code or OpenAPI specs.
evals.expected_output: Creates .claude/skills/api-docs/SKILL.md with proper frontmatter in the shared-skills repo

assertions.text: Description is trigger-focused (mentions when to use)
